content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 15,
1994
John H.
Hardee
Attorney at Law
13305 Penn Street,
Suite 100
Whittier,
CA 90602—1728
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1-94—159
Dear Mr. Hardee:
You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (“the Act”).
1
!
Since your
letter is a general inquiry,
we consider it to be a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) •2/
QUESTION
You have asked who should be the conflict of interest code
reviewing body for the Southeast Area Social Services Funding
Authority (“Authority”)
and where disclosure forms should be
obtained and filed?
CONCLUS ION
The code reviewing body for the Authority is the Los Angeles
County Board of Supervisors.
The statements of economic interests
(Form 730) required to be filed after approval of the conflict of
interest code will be supplied by Los Angeles County.
As the code
reviewing body, the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors will
determine where the statements of economic interests are filed.
1!
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2!
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal .written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Mr.
Hardee
File No.
1—94—159
Page Two
FACTS
The Authority is governed by a joint powers agreement between
the County of Los Angeles and several cities.
The board is
comprised of a member appointed by the County Board of Supervisors
and one member from the city council of each participating city.
The Authority administers federal funds, state funds,
county
funds and,
in some cases, city funds.
One of the programs
administered by the Authority is the Federal Job Training
Partnership Act
(“JTPA”).
Your letter states that although the
Authority is primarily in Los Angeles County,
JTPA can cross
county lines.
The amended regulations of JTPA require the
Authority to have a conflict of interest code/policy.
After
researching the Political Reform Act,
you believe the Authority is
required to adopt and promulgate a conflict of interest code under
state requirements in addition to the JTPA requirement.
During our telephone conversation on June 2,
1994,
you
clarified that all of the cities participating in the joint powers
agreement are located in Los Angeles County.
In addition,
you
stated that the Authority provides services to youth in Los
Angeles County; however,
some of the employers of the youth may be
outside of Los Angeles County.
ANALYSIS
The Act provides that every agency shall adopt and promulgate
a conflict of interest code.
(Section 87300.)
The Act further
states that no conflict of interest code shall be effective until
it has been approved by the code reviewing body.
(Section 87303.)
The code reviewing body for any local government agency with
jurisdiction wholly within one county is the board of supervisors.
(Section 82011.)
The Authority is governed by a joint powers agreement between
the County of Los Angeles and several cities located wholly within
Los Angeles County.
The Authority provides services to youth
within Los Angeles County,
although some of the youths’
employers
may be outside Los Angeles County.
In determining whether an
agency principally located within one county has jurisdiction in
another county,
we look at whether the agency owns real property
and to what extent it has authority in another county.
(Woliver
Advice Letter,
Our File No. A-84-123.)
Based on the information you provided, it appears that the
Authority does not own real property outside Los Angeles County,
nor do any of the participants in the joint powers agreement


Mr. Hardee
File No.
94—159
Page Three
extend into any other county.
The fact that some employers
employ youths outside Los Angeles County as part of the JTPA
program would not constitute significant authority in another
county since the beneficiaries of the services are within Los
Angeles County.
Therefore,
we conclude that the Los Angeles
County Board of Supervisors is the Authority’s appropriate code
reviewing body.
The Board can provide guidance for notice and
adoption procedures they may have developed.
As the code reviewing body, the Board of Supervisors will
determine the place of filing for the statements of economic
interests
(Form 730)
as provided in the Authority’s conflict of
interest code.
(Section 87500(0).)
After approval of the
conflict of interest code, the filing officer/ for the statements
will supply the necessary forms and manuals prescribed by the
Commission to the Authority.
(Section 81010.)
Although we do not have all of the required information to
conduct an in—depth review of the proposed conflict of interest
code you submitted with your letter,
we have conducted a cursory
review and find the following revisions are necessary:
The place of filing paragraph on the cover page needs to
be amended to reflect that the statements of economic
interests will be filed with the filing officer as determined
by the Board of Supervisors.
The Fair Political Practices
Commission will not be the filing officer for these
statements.
The appendix of the code needs to contain a list of
designated positions.
Every conflict of interest code must
specifically enumerate the positions within an agency which
involve the making or participation in the making of
decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any
financial interest.
(Section 87302.)
The term designated
employee means any officer,
employee,
member or consultant of
any agency whose position with the agency is designated in a
conflict of interest code because the position entails the
making or participating in the making of decisions which may
foreseeably have a material financial effect on any financial
interest.
(Section 82019.)
Enclosed is information to assist
you in determining who should be designated in the
Authority’s code.
3/
“Filing Officer” means the office or officer with whom the
original statements are required to be filed.
(Section 82027.)


Mr. Hardee
File No.
1—94—159
Page Four
Once a determination has been made as to which positions
should be designated in the code, disclosure categories must
be assigned to the positions based on their job duties and
level of decision making authority.
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Dixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
